posts.title: Q4 Report: Honest Attribution Insights | Wes Parker posted on the ...
posts.author: Wesleyparkerppc
posts.topic: ... 2026 ... Heidrick & Struggles. 570,532 followers. 1w Edited ... Manager - Business Development | Driving Scalable RPO & Executive Hiring Solutions Across GCC
posts.ready_comment: Healthcare and financial services in the GCC are going to face serious pressure in 2026 to find technology leaders who can operate across both transformation strategy and execution, not just one or the other. Having led a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals at Saudi German Hospital Group, I saw firsthand how rare it is to find executives who can bridge clinical operations, regulatory complexity, and modern tech architecture simultaneously. The talent gap at the intersection of domain expertise and digital leadership is only widening, especially as Saudi Vision 2030 initiatives accelerate timelines across sectors. What patterns are you seeing in how organizations are redefining the criteria for senior technology roles in health and fintech specifically?

posts.title: India Market Entry: EOR & Interim Leadership Strategies
posts.author: Interimmanagementworldwide
posts.topic: #Smallconsultancies together can stand #shoulder-to-shoulder with: Korn Ferry | Randstad | McKinsey | Bain | Spencer Stuart | Heidrick &
posts.ready_comment: The real advantage smaller consultancies have is speed and genuine specialization, not just cost. Having led a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals, I consistently found that agile, focused partners outperformed the larger firms on execution when complexity was high and timelines were tight. The boutiques that win long-term are the ones who resist the temptation to generalize just to chase bigger contracts. What's your view on how smaller firms should position their specialization without underselling their strategic depth?
posts.comment_angle: #Smallconsultancies together can stand #shoulder-to-shoulder with: Korn Ferry |

posts.title: GCC Engineering Demand Forecast 2026
posts.author: Maged Mahmoud Naeem
posts.topic: ... Digital Engineering Specialists Share of Demand: 3% – 6% Estimated Demand: 5000 – 16000 Engineers Digital transformation, BIM adoption, and
posts.ready_comment: Digital transformation demand in engineering is real, but the talent gap is even more pronounced when you try to scale it across complex, multi-site environments. Leading a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals taught me that the shortage isn't just technical skills, it's people who can bridge clinical or operational context with digital execution. BIM adoption in engineering faces the same translation problem that HealthTech does: the specialists exist, but organizations struggle to embed them where decisions actually get made. What strategies are you seeing work best for closing that gap in the Egyptian and GCC markets?

posts.title: iFact Partners with EnnovaTech to Drive Saudi Digital Transformation
posts.author: Ifactsa
posts.topic: Today, iFact formalised a strategic partnership with EnnovaTech Solutions, reinforcing our position as a driving force in the Kingdom's digital
posts.ready_comment: Partnerships like this one succeed or fail based on how well both sides align on execution, not just vision. Having led a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals at Saudi German Hospital Group, I saw firsthand how quickly strategic agreements unravel when the operational layer isn't built with the same rigor as the deal itself. The Vision 2030 digital push is creating real opportunity for partnerships that go beyond press releases and actually move the needle on implementation. What does the integration roadmap between iFact and EnnovaTech look like in the first 90 days?
